solution.p: There are a few ways to solve quadratic equations- the easiest way to solve this particular type of problem is to use the square root method.

p.m: \displaystyle{ \begin{aligned} 27 - 6 ( x - 9 )^2 \amp = 3 \\ 27 - 6 ( x - 9 )^2 \mathbf{{}-27} \amp = 3 \mathbf{{}-27} \\ - 6 ( x - 9 )^2 \amp = -24 \\ \frac{- 6 ( x - 9 )^2}{-6} \amp = \frac{-24}{-6} \\ ( x - 9 )^2 \amp = 4 \\ \sqrt{( x - 9 )^2} \amp = \pm\sqrt{4} \\ x - 9 \amp = \pm 2 \\ x - 9 \mathbf{{} + 9} \amp = \pm 2 \mathbf{{} + 9} \\ x \amp = 2 + 9 \text{ or } x=-2 + 9 \\ x \amp = 11 \text{ or } x=7 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: Checking solutions is left as exercise.
